briefing.headline: Go small, go slow — spawn muddy conditions demand finesse presentations.
briefing.gameplan:
In spawn with muddy water and pre frontal conditions, finesse presentations shine.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast pa
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, dock, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Switch to Ned rig (1/4-oz head, same baits as dropshot) when smallmouth are going down and nipping the dropshot weight instead of the bait
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, dock, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Don't assume you must finesse spawning bass. At the 2011 Classic, 4 of top 7 finishers power fished spinnerbaits and crankbaits on the same 150-yard spawning flat. Key conditions: hard bottom flat with stumps + grass, 10-degree water temp ri...
Yamamoto pro Fukae: Uses 14-lb YGK sinking braid to an 8-lb fluorocarbon leader for spawn-time drop-shot and Ned Rig presentations on a Shimano Expride 170M with 2500-size spinning reel

Johnston: Use Garmin Perspective Mode when seeing bed fish in low-visibility conditions. This was one of two main tools (along with flipping and drop shot baits) for bed fishing at the St. Johns River.

Wheeler: Smallmouth spawn starts in creeks and flat areas with no current, then they transition toward the main river/lake — same as largemouth. Look for spawners in 10-15 ft rather than the obvious 4-8 ft most people target

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal spawning bass are active on beds in 2-5ft. Falling pressure often triggers new waves of fish to move onto beds. Target hard-bottom spawning flats, secondary pockets, and protected coves.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
